item.description: President Trump sits down for an exclusive interview with Meet the Press as the U.S. looks to make a deal with Iran to end the war.

media:content.media:title: Exclusive: Trump says Iran hasn't made a deal because ‘they’re strong’ and ‘proud’
media:content.media:description: President Trump sits down for an exclusive interview with Meet the Press as the U.S. looks to make a deal with Iran to end the war.

item.title: Exclusive: Trump says Iran hasn't made a deal because ‘they’re strong’ and ‘proud’

item.description: Speaking at the St. Petersburg Economic Forum, President Putin suggested he should be addressing his frontline troops rather than the author of the letter, saying to them, “Keep working, brothers.”

media:content.media:title: ‘I see no point in meeting’: Putin dismisses Zelenskyy’s offer of talks
media:content.media:description: Speaking at the St. Petersburg Economic Forum, President Putin suggested he should be addressing his frontline troops rather than the author of the letter, saying to them, “Keep working, brothers.”

item.title: ‘I see no point in meeting’: Putin dismisses Zelenskyy’s offer of talks

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Brennan Leach
Sahil Kapur
item.description: The Senate passed legislation early Friday to fund President Donald Trump’s immigration enforcement agencies after intense bipartisan backlash over a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund threatened to derail the bill.

media:content.media:text: The Capitol Building
media:content.media:description: The Capitol on June 3, 2026.

item.title: Senate passes $70B immigration bill, without limits on ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
Pilar Melendez
Daniel Arkin
item.description: “60 Minutes” correspondents Leslie Stahl, Bill Whitaker and Jon Wertheim said Friday that they planned to stay on at the newsmagazine, capping days of turmoil.

media:content.media:text: Jon Wertheim, Bill Whitaker, Lesley Stahl.
media:content.media:description: “60 Minutes” correspondents Jon Wertheim, Bill Whitaker and Lesley Stahl.

item.title: Lesley Stahl, Bill Whitaker and Jon Wertheim say they’ll stay at ‘60 Minutes’

item.description: British actor Anthony Head, best known for his roles in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer” and “Ted Lasso” has died at the age of 72 due to complications from pneumonia, his family said.

media:content.media:title: Actor Anthony Head dies at age 72
media:content.media:description: British actor Anthony Head, best known for his roles in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer” and “Ted Lasso” has died at the age of 72 due to complications from pneumonia, his family said.

item.title: Actor Anthony Head dies at age 72

item.description: Astronauts are back on board the International Space Station after NASA issued an order to shelter in place and prepare for evacuation as Russia attempted to fix a crack on its side of the station. NBC News' Ton Costello reports on the incident and what comes next in the repairs.

media:content.media:title: Astronauts back on board International Space Station after shelter in place order
media:content.media:description: Astronauts are back on board the International Space Station after NASA issued an order to shelter in place and prepare for evacuation as Russia attempted to fix a crack on its side of the station. NBC News' Ton Costello reports on the incident and what comes next in the repairs.

item.title: Astronauts back on board International Space Station after shelter in place order

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Curaçao will make its World Cup debut this month as the smallest nation ever to qualify for the tournament, thanks to its connection to the Netherlands.

media:content.media:text: Curacao team.
media:content.media:description: The Curaçao team huddles during a friendly match against Scotland in Glasgow on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Curacao fans.
media:content.media:description: The Curaçao team huddles during a friendly match against Scotland in Glasgow on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Curacao team.
media:content.media:description: The Curaçao team huddles during a friendly match against Scotland in Glasgow on Saturday.

item.title: The smallest nation ever to qualify for the World Cup didn’t do it alone

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, we dive into the Democratic response to the latest Graham Platner controversy and the questions that still surround Rep. Tom Kean Jr.’s absence.

media:content.media:text: Senator Bernie Sanders Holds Fighting Oligarchy Event With Senate Candidate Graham Platner.
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner on May 25, 2026.

item.title: Democratic dread in Maine: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said Iranian leaders have not yet reached a deal with the U.S. to end the ongoing war because they’re “strong” and “proud.”

media:content.media:text: Meet The Press- President Trump
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.

item.title: Trump says Iran has not agreed to a U.S. deal because ‘they’re strong, they’re proud’

item.dc:creator: Berkeley Lovelace Jr.
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda could lead to more than 20,000 cases and over 2,000 deaths in a worst-case scenario, according to a CDC report.

media:content.media:text: Workers from the Uganda Red Cross Society evacuate the body of a suspected Ebola victim in Kampala on May 26, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Workers from the Uganda Red Cross Society evacuate the body of a suspected Ebola victim on May 26.

item.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo could top 20,000 cases in worst case, CDC says

item.dc:creator: Kelly O'Donnell
item.description: Security for the FIFA World Cup is expanding skyward, with law enforcement agencies preparing for drone activity that could range from a nuisance to a serious threat as the United States hosts the tournament starting next week.

media:content.media:text: A blue and white drone hovering with FBI written on the bottom
media:content.media:description: An FBI drone hovers during a demonstration by law enforcement in Huntsville, Ala. last month.

media:content.media:text: An aerial photo of a large sports stadium in Mexico City.
media:content.media:description: An FBI drone hovers during a demonstration by law enforcement in Huntsville, Ala. last month.

item.title: FBI warns drone operators to stay away from World Cup matches

item.dc:creator: Erika Edwards
item.description: Measles cases in the U.S. reached 2,030 on Friday, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reported.

media:content.media:text: Measles Outbreak Explainer
media:content.media:description: A health worker talks to people before administering measles tests outside Seminole Hospital District, Feb. 21, 2025, in Seminole, Texas.

item.title: Measles is still spreading in the U.S., with more than 2,000 cases this year

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Samantha Busch spoke out publicly about the sudden death of her husband, two-time NASCAR Cup Series champion Kyle Busch, on Instagram on Friday.“The prayers, messages, flowers, meals, hugs, and countless acts of kindness have carried us through the most heartbreaking days of our lives,” she said.

media:content.media:text: A somber grouping of people at a racetrack wearing black caps with the number 8 on them.
media:content.media:description: Samantha Busch, widow of Kyle Busch, and their son, Brexton Busch, stand on the grid during the remembrance ceremony for Kyle Busch at Charlotte Motor Speedway in Concord, N.C., on May 24.

item.title: Samantha Busch says family feels ‘not alone’ in first statement since Kyle’s death

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
Tim Rohan
Sahil Kapur
item.description: In Game 2 of the Stanley Cup Final, the Vegas Golden Knights held a 2-0 lead entering the third period and seemed poised to take control of this series.

media:content.media:text: Jordan Staal #11 of the Carolina Hurricanes celebrates his goal with teammates during the third period of Game Two of the Stanley Cup Final between the Vegas Golden Knights and the Carolina Hurricanes at Lenovo Center on June 04, 2026 in Raleigh, North Carolina.
media:content.media:description: Carolina Hurricanes' Jordan Staal celebrates his goal during the third period of Game Two of the Stanley Cup Final on June 4.

media:content.media:text: Seth Jarvis #24 of the Carolina Hurricanes celebrates after scoring the game-winning goal in overtime to defeat the Vegas Golden Knights in Game Two of the 2026 Stanley Cup Final at Lenovo Center on June 04, 2026 in Raleigh, North Carolina.
media:content.media:description: Carolina Hurricanes' Jordan Staal celebrates his goal during the third period of Game Two of the Stanley Cup Final on June 4.

media:content.media:credit: Chris Brunskill / Fantasista via Getty Images File
media:content.media:text: Viktor Gyokeres of Arsenal celebrates scoring a penalty to make the score 0-1 during the UEFA Champions League 2025/26 Semi Final First Leg match between Atletico de Madrid and Arsenal FC at on April 29, 2026 in Madrid, Spain.
media:content.media:description: Carolina Hurricanes' Jordan Staal celebrates his goal during the third period of Game Two of the Stanley Cup Final on June 4.

media:content.media:text: Mercedes' Italian driver Kimi Antonelli celebrates on the podium his victory in the 2026 Formula 1 Grand Prix du Canada at Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve in Montreal, Canada, on May 24, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Carolina Hurricanes' Jordan Staal celebrates his goal during the third period of Game Two of the Stanley Cup Final on June 4.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Carolina storms back to beat Vegas, even Stanley Cup Final

item.dc:creator: Erika Edwards
item.description: The World Cup is presenting a unique challenge for public health officials.

media:content.media:text: Manchester United v West Ham United FC: Premier League Summer Series
media:content.media:description: Inside the stadium during the Premier League Summer Series at MetLife Stadium on July 26, 2025 in East Rutherford, N.J.

item.title: Why measles and flu — not Ebola — are front of mind for doctors ahead of the World Cup

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Major stock indexes fell sharply after a strong jobs report set the stage for the Federal Reserve to hike rates.

media:content.media:text: NYSE traders.
media:content.media:description: Traders work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange at the opening bell on May 27.

item.title: Stocks sink as worries about an interest rate hike rattle tech investors

item.dc:creator: Samantha Cookinham
David K. Li
item.description: British actor Anthony Head, known to generations of American TV viewers through polar opposite characters named Rupert on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer” and “Ted Lasso,” has died, his family said Friday

media:content.media:text: 1998 Anthony Stewart Head stars in "Buffy the Vampire Slayer."
media:content.media:description: Anthony Stewart Head stars in "Buffy the Vampire Slayer."

media:content.media:text: "Buffy The Vampire Slayer"
media:content.media:description: From left, David Boreanaz, Nicholad Head, Alyson Hannigan, Seth Green, Sarah Michelle Gellar, Nicholas Brendon and Charisma Carpenter in "Buffy The Vampire Slayer."

media:content.media:text: LITTLE BRITAIN
media:content.media:description: Anthony Head as the prime minister with David Walliams in "Little Britain."

media:content.media:text: Anthony Head in "Ted Lasso."
media:content.media:description: Anthony Head in "Ted Lasso."

item.title: Anthony Head, ‘Buffy the Vampire Slayer’ and ‘Ted Lasso’ actor, dies at 72

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: We’re tracking the best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals, including discounts on tech, home, beauty, kitchen products and more before the four-day sale begins.

media:content.media:text: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.
media:content.media:description: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.

media:content.media:text: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.
media:content.media:description: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.

item.title: The 51+ best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals you can shop now: Bissell, Le Creuset, Levi’s, and more

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: Target just announced its dates for Target Circle Week also called Target Circle Deal Days. The sale event will take place fromTuesday, June 23 to Friday, June 26.

media:content.media:text: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.
media:content.media:description: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.

media:content.media:text: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.
media:content.media:description: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.

item.title: Target just announced dates for Target Circle Week: Here’s everything you should know

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: Le Creuset Dutch ovens, stainless steel pans, nonstick ceramic skillets and more are up to 40% off at the brand’s Summer Cookware Event.

media:content.media:text: Le Creuset’s cookware is known for its sturdiness and excellent heat retention.
media:content.media:description: Le Creuset’s cookware is known for its sturdiness and excellent heat retention.

item.title: Le Creuset Summer Sale: Save $170 on Dutch ovens and up to 40% on nonstick pans and more

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening from June 23 to 26, and only Prime members get access. Here’s what you need to know and what’s new about the sale this year.

media:content.media:text: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.
media:content.media:description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.

media:content.media:text: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.
media:content.media:description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.

item.title: When is Amazon Prime Day 2026? The official dates, plus more about the 4-day sale

item.dc:creator: Tim Stelloh
item.description: The former IRS agent convicted in a deadly catfishing scheme targeting his wife and a stranger was sentenced Friday to a mandatory life term in prison

media:content.media:text: A tilted view of Brendan Banfield, seated in a courtroom.
media:content.media:description: Brendan Banfield in Fairfax County Circuit Court in Fairfax, Va., in January.

item.title: Brendan Banfield receives life sentence in elaborate au pair double murder plot

item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa
Maria Guerrero, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth
Meredith Yeomans, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth
item.description: Tensions were high as the second day of testimony in the murder trial of a Texas teenager accused of stabbing to death a high schooler at a track meet got underway Friday.

media:content.media:text: Track Meet Student Killed
media:content.media:description: Supporters for Karmelo Anthony demonstrate in front of the Collin County courthouse Thursday, June 4, 2026, in McKinney, Texas.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony jury views video showing chaotic aftermath of fatal stabbing

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
Nathan Gonzalez
Corky Siemaszko
item.description: ALEXANDRIA, Va. — The former CIA officer found with $40 million in gold bars stashed at his Virginia home was ordered held behind bars Friday until he goes on trial for criminal theft of public money after a prosecutor branded him a “master manipulator.”David Rush, a senior CIA officer who worked on one of the most highly sensitive programs in the U.S. government, watched as prosecutors and his defense attorney sparred in a Virginia courtroom over whether he should remain in jail

media:content.media:text: Former CIA Officer David Rush appears in court on June 5, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Former CIA Officer David Rush appears in court on June 5, 2026.

item.title: Former CIA officer accused of stashing gold bars ordered to remain in jail pending trial
